interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, working in mature SaaS
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design an email send pipeline handling 100M emails per day
Design a template engine with personalization at scale

coding: At Mailchimp, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Mailchimp scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, working in mature saas.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design an email send pipeline handling 100M emails per day.
Design a template engine with personalization at scale.
values: Mailchimp succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Mailchimp compare to Klaviyo or HubSpot?
What is the engineering culture under Intuit?

Mailchimp Interview Guide (2026): Email Marketing Platform

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Mailchimp
mailchimp.com ↗

Mailchimp is the dominant SMB email marketing platform — owned by Intuit since 2021. The interview is email-platform-flavored with focus on deliverability at massive scale (billions of emails monthly), template engines, and SMB-friendly automation.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design an email send pipeline handling 100M emails per day

- Design a template engine with personalization at scale

- Design segmentation and audience filtering

- Coding: medium DSA, often with batch-processing flavor

- Behavioral: customer focus, working in mature SaaS

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $135K–$170K total

- Senior SE: $200K–$260K

- Staff: $290K–$380K

- Principal: $400K–$520K

Intuit comp bands apply post-acquisition; below FAANG.

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in PHP (legacy core) or Java/Go (newer services)

- Understand email deliverability: SPF, DKIM, DMARC, sender reputation

- Brush up on segmentation and audience analytics patterns

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Mailchimp remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Atlanta (HQ), Brooklyn, Oakland. Some engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Mailchimp compare to Klaviyo or HubSpot?

Mailchimp is SMB-focused; Klaviyo is e-commerce-specific; HubSpot is enterprise CRM. Mailchimp pays mid-tier; Klaviyo is closer to FAANG.

What is the engineering culture under Intuit?

Stable, predictable, mature. Less expansive than peak independent days; more enterprise-process-friendly.
